title: Frame Budgets, Profiling, Allocations, Collision Cost, and Responsiveness · Contract Workshop
summary: A two-person studio incrementally builds representative frame-time and capacity evidence from original geometric fixtures while retaining earlier Python, repository, determinism, frame-budget, accessibility, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of representative frame-time and capacity evidence while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Allocate a target frame budget across event, simulation, collision, rendering, presentation, and audio work using percentiles.
Profile warm representative waves, worst supported entity counts, stalls, resize, pause, and low-power targets before optimizing.
Measure per-frame allocations and cache immutable assets, transforms, masks, text, or pools only when evidence justifies complexity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Frame Budget
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Asteroids team handles case 2882 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to profile update and render phases against percentile frame-time, allocation, entity, collision, and responsiveness budgets without changing behavior; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Allocate a target frame budget across event, simulation, collision, rendering, presentation, and audio work using percentiles.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Frame Budget.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for allocate a target frame budget across event, simulation, collision, rendering, presentation, and audio work using percentiles. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Frame Budget, keep Frame Budget connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.text: A workshop Asteroids team handles case 2168 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to profile update and render phases against percentile frame-time, allocation, entity, collision, and responsiveness budgets without changing behavior; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Allocate a target frame budget across event, simulation, collision, rendering, presentation, and audio work using percentiles.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Performance Regression
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-performance-regression-5ghnqn" that uses case 8456 to profile update and render phases against percentile frame-time, allocation, entity, collision, and responsiveness budgets without changing behavior. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Performance Regression.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Performance Regression into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Performance Regression, keep Performance Regression connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-performance-regression-5ghnqn
# Competency: asteroids-performance-regression.
# Case 8456: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death.
def evidence_asteroids_performance_regression_5ghnqn(updates: int = 120, draws: int = 60, frame_p95_ms: float = 12.4, frame_budget_ms: float = 16.667, allocations_per_frame: int = 3, allocation_budget: int = 8, output_equal: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_5ghnqn = "5ghnqn-nqnhg5-8456"
    assert evidence_variant_5ghnqn
    evidence_axes_5ghnqn = (
        "player5ghnqn",
        "seed8456",
        "inputnqnhg5",
        "frame5ghnqn8456",
        "state84565ghnqn",
        "playtestnqnhg58456",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_5ghnqn)) == 6
    if updates < 1 or draws < 1 or frame_p95_ms < 0.0:
        return False, frame_p95_ms, "profile-domain"
    if frame_p95_ms > frame_budget_ms:
        return False, frame_p95_ms, "frame-budget"
    if allocations_per_frame > allocation_budget:
        return False, allocations_per_frame, "allocation-budget"
    if not output_equal:
        return False, frame_p95_ms, "semantic-regression"
    assert frame_p95_ms <= frame_budget_ms
    return True, round(frame_p95_ms, 3), "profiled-frame"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Performance Regression
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Asteroids team handles case 7930 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to profile update and render phases against percentile frame-time, allocation, entity, collision, and responsiveness budgets without changing behavior; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Gate percentile frame time, input-to-feedback latency, memory, load time, and semantic replay equivalence by target.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Performance Regression condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
